mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, and others. It provides a straightforward way to deploy and interact with these models via a command-line interface and REST API. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the flexibility of custom deployments.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface (CLI) on your local machine, which is necessary to interact with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster where Ollama will be deployed. Adjust the node count and region as needed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command sets up your kubeconfig for the Ollama cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Ollama application using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/charts

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Deploy Ollama using the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Ollama deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Verify that all Ollama pods are in the Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This command will remove the Ollama release.
> This command will delete the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade the Ollama deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade the Ollama deployment to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ollama image is not available or there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure the image exists on Docker Hub.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ollama pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ollama' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes API server unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --zone=us-central1-a' to reset kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including MiniMax and DeepSeek.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n ollama' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
